3 min read
Just now

--

We paid for this lesson once. No need to pay for it twice.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Ten years ago, the cloud bill caught almost everyone off guard.
Not because anyone did anything wrong. Because the model changed faster than the surrounding controls.
Think about how tech spend used to work. You bought servers. You approved a budget. Costs moved at the speed of a purchase order. Slow, but predictable. You always knew roughly what next quarter looked like.
Then the cloud arrived and handed every engineer a tap. Spin up capacity in minutes. No purchase order. No sign-off. Brilliant for speed. Less brilliant for the person who has to explain the invoice.
Here is what actually happened. Spending decisions moved out of finance and into hundreds of small technical choices, made daily, by people who never saw the total.
The result was the same story everywhere. The bill crept up, month after month. Finance asked why. And nobody could give a straight answer, because the spending was scattered across thousands of decisions, and no single person could see the whole picture.
Out of that mess, a whole discipline appeared. We call it FinOps. Visibility, ownership, forecasting and accountability, bolted onto…
